title: staff — retention windows
description: Active + on_leave + suspended staff never expire. Offboarded staff enter a two-phase retention: 90-day grace hold on emergency_contact_* + tax_id_last4 (GDPR Art. 5(1)(c) minimisation), then 7-year retention on the employment metadata (SOC 2 CC6.3 + national tax + labour law). Compensation history rows retain 7 years even after the parent Staff is hard-deleted — migrated to a compliance-archive table for the residual window. Coaches retain in lockstep with the parent Staff (a coach cannot outlive its parent).
compliance_regimes_referenced: gdpr Art. 5(1)(c) — data minimisation (emergency contact + tax_id_last4 redaction at 90 days)
gdpr Art. 17 — right to erasure (with financial-retention Art. 17(3)(b) exception)
soc2 CC6.3 — segregation of duties audit trail retention
national tax law — 7-year employment-record retention (US, EU, UK, CA baselines)
national labour law — pay-equity + wrongful-termination claim windows (typically 3-6 years, we retain 7)

retention.entity: Staff — active / pre_hire / on_leave / suspended / offboarding

purge.notes: Staff in non-terminal states are the living roster. Retention only applies once employment_status='offboarded'.

purge.action: field-level redaction (replace with [REDACTED]) via PurgeOffboardedStaffJob phase 1
purge.notes: Preserves the rest of the row for the 7-year employment record. Only the confidential PII sub-fields are redacted. The audit trail keeps the pre-redaction values in the audits table with 7-year retention (regulated-access only).

purge.trigger: > 7 years past ended_at + no downstream references (Coach hard-purged, PayrollLine (Wave 4) hard-purged)
purge.notes: RESTRICT FKs catch active references; the job records `retention_blocked` metric when hard-delete is refused. Retention runs against ended_at, not deleted_at, because the employment record's compliance significance is dated from termination.

cold.days: 2555 (7 years) — outlives the parent Staff hard-purge if necessary

purge.notes: PARENT-INDEPENDENT retention. When a tenant is erased (TenantErased), compensation_history rows migrate to a compliance-archive table (retained in encrypted form by ops for the residual 7-year window). This is a regulator-compliance requirement — pay-equity claims can look back up to 6-7 years even after the tenant has fully offboarded from Stackra.

purge.trigger: > 7 years past coach_ended_at + parent Staff hard-purged
purge.notes: A Coach cannot be hard-purged before its parent Staff. RESTRICT FK enforces this at the DB layer.

purge.action: cascaded via the coach row (certifications are a JSONB sub-field, not a separate table)
purge.notes: Historical certification data (add/remove/expire events) is inspectable via the audit trail's before/after snapshots on CoachCertificationAdded / CoachCertificationExpired / CoachCertificationRemoved events.

cross_reference: The audit rows written by Auditable outlive the source rows per audit::retention.json — audit retention is 7 years regardless of source-row retention. This applies to normal audits + the dedicated staff_compensation_history stream.

TenantErased cascades hard-delete of every Staff + Coach row via FK CASCADE, but the audit rows + compensation_history rows survive the cascade (per audit module's outlives-source design + the compliance-archive migration for compensation).
